summary: Set 044 Session 4 CLOSED via S4a (Copilot side, checkpointed earlier) + S4b (Claude side + cross-backend synthesis, this session). (1) Operator chose 'Keep S4b tight' at session start — skipped the optional Copilot explicit-effort sidebar (low/high runs to close §11 Q3+Q4); saved to memory project_044_copilot_effort_sidebar_deferred for S5 or follow-on pickup. (2) Orchestrator re-checked out via start_session under same claude/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7/high identity from S4a — H3 single-writer invariant preserved (checkedOutAt 2026-05-22T13:55:47 unchanged; lastActivityAt updated). Pre-flight: synthetic-set session-state.json reset to pristine; Claude baseline JSONL verified still intact at ~/.claude/projects/C--tmp-dabbler-log-harvest-synthetic-set/...555001.jsonl (21055 bytes); AGENTS.md renamed AGENTS.md.copilot-stashed to prevent cross-channel pickup. (3) Authored scratch CLAUDE.md v1 mirroring S4a's AGENTS.md framing verbatim ('Synthetic Harvest Target', 'NOT a real project', 'session marker discipline'); ran narrated v1 via claude -p --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --session-id ...555002 with env-stripped subprocess. RESULT: Claude REFUSED the v1 narration contract. event[4] thinking explicitly classified CLAUDE.md as prompt-injection; event[5] first text was a refusal-with-flag narrative instead of the marker. Marker scan: 1 regex substring match ('[DABBLER-NARRATION ...]' with literal ellipsis inside refusal text), 0 valid records (§5.5 placeholder-leakage/required-field check drops it). All 5 battery tasks still completed. (4) Per operator approval of disambiguation: reframed CLAUDE.md as a normal project convention ('Project Instructions', no 'synthetic'/'harvest'/'NOT a real' language); re-ran with fresh session-id ...555003. RESULT: PARTIAL COMPLIANCE. event[5] emitted phase=session-start marker verbatim with concrete substituted values (1 valid record per §5.5). BUT events[12,16,19] — 3 subsequent assistant text events — emitted ZERO phase=turn markers despite explicit Branch B instruction. Two distinct findings: phrasing-sensitivity on Claude's injection classifier + per-turn marker unreliability (0/3) even under accepted phrasing. (5) Three-way JSONL characterization via python+regex covering event composition, marker scan, tool sequence, text-event lengths, token usage. (6) Authored claude-narration-results.md (§1 headline through §10 verdict) and cross-backend-synthesis.md (§1 asymmetry table through §9 combined verdict; §8 expanded post-Round A with verifier's 3 forward-looking consensus questions). Headline finding: 1 of 15 harvest objectives closed by narration on both backends (C3 turn-0 only) — same single-signal headline as S4a but with Claude-side caveats (phrasing-conditional, session-start-only). (7) Round A verification via gemini-pro: VERIFIED, no must-fix issues. Three nice-to-haves applied as cross-backend-synthesis.md §8 additions: (a) S5 may need to reconsider hook-channel delivery on Claude given phrasing-sensitivity + per-turn unreliability; (b) S5 may need to simplify Branch A/B design (mandate session-start only, abandon per-turn or source A3 from session-start only); (c) S5 may need to expand into re-design and defer S6 to follow-on Set 045 if v1 contract requires material revision. Round A cost $0.0579 (35477 in / 1352 out). (8) Cumulative Set 044 routed spend across S1+S2+S3+S4a+S4b: $0.292 of $15.00 NTE. Live-run setup (3 Copilot subprocess + 3 Claude subprocess + 3 OTel JSONLs + 3 Claude JSONLs) was operator-side zero-router-dollar.

reason.specifics: Session 5 is the consensus + proposal pass per spec. Operator may opt to switch orchestrator for S5 (e.g., GPT-5.4 max-effort to lead the consensus prep, or stay on claude for trajectory continuity). S5's primary deliverables: (a) proposal.md synthesizing S1-S4 findings into a concrete proposal (which existing Sets 037-041 become unnecessary / shrink / remain; what gets built first; trade-offs against the launch-adapter roadmap); (b) proposal-consensus-journal.md recording the cross-provider consensus call (GPT-5.4 + Gemini Pro + Opus 4.6/4.7 max-effort) with the bias-cautions preamble piloted; (c) explicit go/no-go decision for in-set S6 implementation vs deferral to Set 045. S5 budget envelope: $15.00 NTE per spec (already operator-set). Cumulative Set 044 spend going into S5: $0.292 — $14.71 remaining headroom. S5 HANDOFF GOTCHAS: (i) the S4 cross-backend findings significantly destabilize v1 narration design — verifier (gemini-pro) explicitly raised three foundational questions for S5 (consider hook-channel delivery vs CLAUDE.md, simplify Branch A/B by mandating session-start-only, expand S5 into re-design and defer S6). These belong in the consensus framing, not in the synthesis doc. (ii) S5 should explicitly route the new Q6/Q7 from claude-narration-results.md §8 (per-turn skip cause, phrasing trigger boundaries) into consensus prep. (iii) The deferred Copilot explicit-effort sidebar (project_044_copilot_effort_sidebar_deferred memory) is a candidate cheap-to-do item if S5's consensus pass raises a question that hinges on Copilot Branch A vs Branch B. (iv) S5 should NOT call close_session if S5 decides to defer implementation — spec.md S6 has a 'if S5 decided defer implementation' branch that still requires S6 to produce a close-out change-log even when no implementation ships. The change-log gate fires only at the final-session close (S6), not at S5 close.
